titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/239

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=207 status=live modified=2017-03-08 13:29:22.92396+00 | translation moa=206 status=live modified=2017-03-08 13:29:20.175074+00

p.cl: Ric’ dei gratia Ecclesie Sancti Andree humilis minister . vniuersis sancte matris ecclesie filiis  Salutem.

Sciant tam
posteri quam presentes nos dedisse  concessisse  hac carta nostra confirmasse Ecclesie Sancte Crucis de
Edenburg .  canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus Ecclesiam de Botheltune tenendam in liberam  per
petuam elemosinam . Quare uolumus  auctoritate episcopali mandamus ut predicti canonici prenominatam
ecclesiam libere teneant . quiete  honorifice possideant cum omnibus ad eam iuste pertinentibus . Saluo
iure episcopali .  Salua tenura Radulfi de Sancto martino.

Hiis testibus . Magistro . A archidiacono Laodonie .
Waltero archidiacono de Sancto Andr’ . Iohanne nepote Roberti episcopi . A . decano Laodonie . Alexandro  Willelmo capellanis
Ricardi episcopi . Galfrido dapifero episcopi . Magistro Abraham . Roberto capellano . Godwino capellano . Dauid
capellano . Hugone pincerna episcopi .  aliis quam pluribus boni testimonii uiris .
Richard by the grace of God humble servant of the church of St Andrews to all sons of Holy Mother Church, Greeting:

Let those to come as well as those present know that we have given and granted and by this our charter made firm to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there, the church of Bolton, to be held in free and perpetual alms. Wherefore I will and by episcopal authority command that the said canons should hold that church freely, quietly and honourably with everything rightfully belonging to it. Reserving episcopal right, and reserving the tenure of Ralph de St-Martin.

With these witnesses, Master Andrew archdeacon of Lothian, Walter archdeacon of St Andrews, John the Bishop Robert’s nephew, Aiulf dean of Lothian, Alexander and William chaplains of Bishop Richard, Geoffrey the bishop’s steward, Master Abraham, Robert the chaplain, Godwin the chaplain, David the chaplain, Hugh the bishop’s butler, and very many other men of good testimony.
